litigation.mdlName: In re: Johnson & Johnson Talcum Powder Products Marketing, Sales Practices and Products Liability Litigation
litigation.court: U.S. District Court, District of New Jersey

phase: Active litigation resuming after the third bankruptcy was rejected
phaseDetail: After J&J's third 'Texas Two-Step' bankruptcy (Red River Talc LLC) was denied on March 31, 2025, previously paused cases returned to active dockets. As of June 1, 2026, MDL-2738 is the largest active MDL in the country by pending actions, and trials are proceeding in state courts. No global settlement is in place.

pendingCounts.scope: Pending actions in MDL-2738 (largest active MDL by actions pending)
pendingCounts.source: JPML, Pending MDL Dockets by Actions Pending, June 1, 2026

defendants: Kenvue Inc. (consumer-health spinoff; Johnson's Baby Powder brand)
LLT Management LLC (formerly LTL Management) / Red River Talc LLC (J&J talc-liability subsidiaries)

settlements.label: No global settlement — three bankruptcy attempts rejected
settlements.detail: J&J's three 'Texas Two-Step' bankruptcy attempts to cap talc liability were all rejected by the courts. Most recently, Red River Talc LLC's prepackaged Chapter 11 — built around a proposed roughly $9 billion plan — was denied by U.S. Bankruptcy Judge Christopher Lopez in the Southern District of Texas on March 31, 2025. No global settlement is in place; ovarian-cancer claims are coordinated in MDL-2738 and individual cases are being tried in state courts.
settlements.source: Drugwatch — coverage of the March 31, 2025 Red River dismissal

keyDates.label: MDL-2738 established
keyDates.detail: The JPML centralized federal Johnson & Johnson talcum-powder cases before Judge Michael A. Shipp in the U.S. District Court for the District of New Jersey.

keyDates.label: Ingham verdict ($4.69B)
keyDates.detail: A St. Louis jury awarded about $4.69 billion to 22 women with ovarian cancer in Ingham v. Johnson & Johnson (Missouri state court).

keyDates.label: Ingham reduced on appeal (~$2.1B)
keyDates.detail: The Missouri Court of Appeals reduced the Ingham judgment to roughly $2.1 billion and trimmed the plaintiff group on jurisdictional grounds.

keyDates.label: U.S. Supreme Court denied review in Ingham
keyDates.detail: The Supreme Court declined to hear J&J's appeal, leaving the reduced Ingham judgment in place as a final result.

keyDates.label: Third bankruptcy (Red River Talc) denied
keyDates.detail: U.S. Bankruptcy Judge Christopher Lopez (S.D. Texas) denied confirmation of Red River Talc's prepackaged Chapter 11 plan, ending J&J's third 'Texas Two-Step.'

keyDates.label: Philadelphia plaintiff verdict (on appeal)
keyDates.detail: A Philadelphia jury found J&J liable in Emerson v. Johnson & Johnson and awarded $250,000; J&J said it would appeal.

sources.name: JPML — Pending MDL Dockets by Actions Pending, June 1, 2026 (PDF)

U.S. Supreme Court — Ingham docket No. 20-1223 (cert. denied June 1, 2021)
Drugwatch — Red River Talc Chapter 11 denied (Mar. 31, 2025)
